label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we know about the mother based on the text. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the story. The mother had five kids and was too busy raising them to have a garden. She mentions that money was tight and time was limited. She used to tell the kids that money doesn't grow on trees. Later, after the kids left home, her son Marvin planted a money tree with $100 bills and helped her create a garden. The garden is now beautiful, and she feels her children are with her when she tends to it. The last sentence says she's not sure if money grows on trees, but she knows love does. Looking at the options: A) She is a bad-tempered woman. The text doesn't mention her being angry or bad-tempered. She was busy and had to say no to her kids' requests, but that's not necessarily bad-tempered. So probably not A. B) She believes money can grow on a tree now. The text says she's still not sure if money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So B is incorrect. C) She is a writer. The story is written in first person, but there's no indication she's a writer. She's a mother who created a garden. So C is probably not right. D) She is hard-working in housekeeping. She had five kids, was too busy to have a garden, and later worked on the garden. She raised the kids with limited money and time, which implies she was hard-working. The fact that she's tending the garden now also shows she's hard-working. So D seems correct. Wait, let me check again. The mother was busy raising the kids, didn't have time to create a garden. She's working in the kitchen when the money tree appears. Marvin helps her dig
